judge_results.call_completion_feedback: The call had a goodbye, but the agent restarted the conversation after ending it, so it didn’t conclude cleanly on the first close.
judge_results.opted_in_feedback: The user indicated it was about the Kijiji vehicle, but there was no explicit confirmation they expected/remembered opting in to be contacted.
judge_results.call_summary_feedback: Seller called about a 2014 Ford Explorer Limited 4WD (~120,000 km). Vehicle in great condition; new brake pads and battery a month ago; clean title/no accidents. Listed at $9,000 and would take $8,000. Available weekends 12–6 PM; prefers Devin to call.
judge_results.all_feedback: The prompt asks for conversational, friend-like language, but the agent often sounded more formal/scripted. It also repeated the exact same line twice (“I’m letting Devin know right now, he’ll be thrilled.”). The agent repeated “I’m letting Devin know right now, he’ll be thrilled.” twice, and later re-engaged after a clear goodbye (“just checking I didn’t lose you there”). Mostly positive and efficient, but the extra post-goodbye follow-up (“still with me?”) made the ending feel slightly awkward/pushy. transcript:
bot: Hi, this is Sarah, calling on behalf of Devin. Is this regarding the vehicle you have for sale on Kijiji? 
human: Yeah. I wanted to know... yeah. Yeah. It's about it.
bot: Oh, absolutely. Devin mentioned he reached out about your vehicle - he asked me to grab a few quick details so he can firm up the best offer. Just so I’m on the right one… what’s the year, make, and model? And about how many kilometers are on it right now? 
human: Uh, the year is, um, twenty fourteen, and it's a Ford Explorer. And, uh, the kilometer is about hundred and twenty kilometers right now, give or take.
bot: Perfect - so a two thousand fourteen Ford Explorer, and you’re at about one hundred and twenty thousand kilometers, give or take. How’s the overall condition - would you say it’s more like excellent, good, fair… and are there any issues or warning lights on right now? 
human: It's, uh, it's working good. There's really nothing wrong with it. It's, uh, the condition is very... is great, actually. I did some repairs on it recently. And, uh, yeah, everything is good.
bot: Got it - sounds like it’s in great shape and running strong, with some recent repairs done. Two quick ones for Devin: What’s your current asking price on it… and are you firm on that, or a little flexible? 
human: My asking price... well, when you say asking price, what do you mean?
bot: Yeah, fair question. I just mean the number you listed it for online - the price you’d be happy to sell it for. What price do you have it posted at right now? 
human: I posted it for about nine thousand dollars. It's... well, I'm open... I'm able to go for, let's say, eight thousand if we find the right buyer.
bot: I'm letting Devin know right now, he'll be thrilled. 
bot: I'm letting Devin know right now, he'll be thrilled. 
bot: Got it - you’ve got it posted at nine thousand, and you’d consider eight thousand for the right buyer. That’s really helpful. Couple more quick things so Devin can price it properly: why are you selling it, and do you know roughly what repairs you did recently ( like brakes, tires, battery, anything like that) ? 
human: I did the brake pads and battery repairs recently about a month ago. Uh, what was the other question?
bot: Perfect - brake pads and a new battery about a month ago. The other thing I asked was: what’s the main reason you’re selling the Explorer? 
human: Well, I'm calling because I found a new vehicle that's consistent my family. So I want to sell this one so I can be able to have cash on hand.
bot: Gotcha - you found something that fits the family better and you want to free up cash for it. Makes total sense. Last couple details for Devin: do you have a clean title, and has it ever been in any accidents or had any insurance claims? 
human: Yeah. I'm totally clean. No accidents.
bot: Love it - clean title and no accidents. Now, to line up the right buyer: are you in Lethbridge, and when would you be available for someone to come take a look… like evenings, weekends, or pretty flexible? Also, what’s the best number to text you at if Devin wants to move fast? 
human: Yeah. You can call me on this number, and I'm available every every weekend Saturday Saturday and Sunday, twelve to six PM.
bot: Perfect - I’ve got you down as available Saturdays and Sundays from twelve noon to six p.m., and we can use this number to reach you. One last quick thing: what trim is it - like XLT, Limited, Sport - and is it four wheel drive or front wheel drive? 
human: It's a four wheel drive, and it's limited.
bot: Awesome - so it’s a two thousand fourteen Ford Explorer Limited, four wheel drive, about one hundred and twenty thousand kilometers, posted at nine thousand and you’d consider eight thousand, clean title, no accidents, new brake pads and battery about a month ago, and you’re free weekends from twelve to six. Devin’s going to review this and get back to you shortly. Do you prefer he texts first or just calls you when he has a buyer lined up? 
human: Yeah. You're gonna call
bot: Perfect - I’ll have Devin call you. If anything changes on your end ( like the price or your weekend availability) , just tell him when he rings. He’ll reach out shortly. 
human: Alright. Sounds good. Thank you very much.
bot: You bet - talk soon. Bye for now. 
bot: Hey, just checking I didn’t lose you there… you still with me? 
human: No. Bye for now. Bye bye.
bot: Okay, bye.
